outbound_official.note: no official Iraq government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Searched mofa.gov.iq (main site, /visa/, /consular-services/, and multiple embassy sub-pages), evisa.iq, epp.iq (Iraqi Electronic Passport portal), and icass.mofa.gov.iq as of 2026-06-14. The Iraqi MFA website covers only visas granted TO foreigners entering Iraq and consular services for Iraqis abroad; no page equivalent to India's mea.gov.in/vffin listing where Iraqi ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa was found on any *.gov.iq domain.

visa_free.notes: Visa waiver agreement between Iraq and Iran. Iran's Ambassador to Baghdad confirmed visas are not required for Iranians arriving through Baghdad and Najaf airports, and for Iraqis travelling to Iran. The Iraqi Embassy in Tehran (mofa.gov.iq/tehran) lists standard visa types without noting this exemption explicitly; the bilateral agreement is confirmed by official statements but the stay duration is not stated in any official source reviewed.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Iraq Ministry of Interior approved granting Visit visas to Malaysian passport holders at points of entry (airports and land borders), effective September 11, 2024. This is a visa on arrival arrangement, not visa-free entry. Stay duration not stated in the official embassy announcement.

e_visa.notes: E-visa mandatory since March 1, 2025. Previously eligible for visa on arrival. Apply at eservice.evisa.iq before travel. The Texas consulate page (official mofa.gov.iq) states valid 60 days from date of arrival. Fee approximately $158 USD (includes mandatory health insurance).

visa_policy.notes: Iraq's official e-visa portal is https://evisa.iq (application system at eservice.evisa.iq). As of March 1, 2025, the Ministry of Interior suspended visa on arrival for 37 countries; these must now obtain e-visa in advance. The confirmed 37 countries are all 27 EU member states plus USA, UK, Russia, China, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, Switzerland, and Norway. The official Iraqi MFA Texas consulate page states the e-visa is valid for 60 days from date of arrival; some secondary sources say 30 days — the official mofa.gov.iq source is taken as authoritative here. E-visa fee approximately $158 USD (206,000 IQD), inclusive of mandatory health insurance, processing 24–48 hours. Malaysia was moved from the prior draft's visa_free consideration to visa_on_arrival per the official mofa.gov.iq Kuala Lumpur embassy announcement (effective September 11, 2024). Lebanon visa-free status for ordinary passports was removed as the mofa.gov.iq Beirut page shows standard visa requirements without any exemption. Turkey and Lebanon ordinary passport exemptions are noted in Wikipedia but were not confirmable on any mofa.gov.iq page and are therefore NOT recorded.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, service, and special passports of China are exempt from entry visa per bilateral agreement signed June 6, 2022. Stay duration not stated in the official mofa.gov.iq announcement.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Serbia exempt from entry visa. Agreement ratified by President of Iraq on November 27, 2019. Stay duration of 90 days per secondary sources; official ratification announcement does not state duration explicitly.

Holders of diplomatic passports of Morocco exempt from entry visa. MoU on mutual visa exemption for diplomatic passport holders signed February 8, 2025 by Iraqi FM Fuad Hussein and Moroccan FM Nasser Bourita. Stay duration not stated in the official announcement.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Indonesia exempt from entry visa. Agreement signed September 23, 2024 by Iraqi Deputy PM/FM Fuad Hussein and Indonesian FM Retno Marsudi on the principle of reciprocity. The mofa.gov.iq announcement confirms signing only; entry-into-force date not specified. Wikipedia notes this may not yet be in force.

cbi.notes: Iraq does not offer a citizenship by investment program. Iraqi citizenship is based on descent or birth in Iraq. No official CBI program found on any official Iraqi government source.

programs.name: Kurdistan Region Investor Residence Permit (Real Estate Investment Pathway)
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who purchase a residential unit within an approved investment project valued at minimum USD 50,000 are eligible for a work residence permit valid for 1–3 years, renewable, upon approval by the Kurdistan Region Board of Investment (gov.krd/boi-en). Extends to spouse, children under 18, and parents. This is a Kurdistan Regional Government program, not a federal Iraq program. The official gov.krd/boi-en page returned HTTP 403 during verification; details sourced from invest.gov.krd.

programs.name: Kurdistan Region Investor Residence Permit (Board of Investment Confirmation Pathway)
programs.notes: Foreign investors confirmed by the Kurdistan Region Board of Investment are eligible for a residency permit valid for 3–5 years, renewable. Extends to spouse, children under 18, and parents. No minimum investment amount specified in the legislation for this pathway. This is a Kurdistan Regional Government program, not a federal Iraq program.

data_quality.validation_notes: Re-validation pass June 2026. Key corrections from prior record: (1) e-visa section expanded from 9 consolidated entries to all 37 individual countries (the full confirmed list: 27 EU member states + USA, UK, Russia, China, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, Switzerland, Norway) — prior record used bloc/grouping entries which was imprecise. (2) Malaysia moved from erroneous implicit visa-free consideration to visa_on_arrival, per mofa.gov.iq Kuala Lumpur embassy announcement effective September 11, 2024 — the announcement explicitly says 'entry visa at points of entry', i.e. visa on arrival, not visa-free. (3) Lebanon and Turkey ordinary-passport visa-free claims removed: mofa.gov.iq Beirut page shows no exemption; no mofa.gov.iq URL confirms ordinary-passport exemption for either. These were only in Wikipedia and are now treated as unverified gaps. (4) China max_stay_days corrected from 30 to null — the mofa.gov.iq announcement (2022/32517) does not state a stay duration; 30-day figure was unconfirmed. (5) The prior visa_free Iran entry had null max_stay_days which remains correct; the Tehran embassy page shows standard visa types with no exemption noted, consistent with prior assessment. (6) Poland added to e-visa list (was missing from prior grouped EU entry count, now listed explicitly).
gaps: Maximum stay duration for the Iran bilateral visa-free arrangement is not stated on any official mofa.gov.iq page reviewed. The Iraq embassy in Tehran lists standard visa procedures without noting the exemption.

The Iraqi MFA Texas consulate (mofa.gov.iq) states e-visa is valid 60 days from arrival; multiple secondary sources say 30 days. The discrepancy between 60 days (official mofa.gov.iq Texas consulate) and 30 days (secondary aggregators, Wikipedia) is unresolved. 60 days used here as the official source.
E-visa fee of approximately $158 USD (including mandatory health insurance) is confirmed by secondary sources; exact fee breakdown is not displayed on the official evisa.iq landing page itself.

Lebanon ordinary passport: Wikipedia and secondary sources claim visa-free entry (up to 6 months for diplomatic / 3 months for service passports, and visa-free for ordinary passports). The mofa.gov.iq Beirut embassy page shows standard visa requirements with no exemption. No mofa.gov.iq bilateral agreement announcement URL found. Not recorded as visa-free.

Turkey ordinary passport: Wikipedia states visa-free for Turks aged under 15 or over 50. The mofa.gov.iq announcement (2025/57778) concerns Iraqi nationals entering Turkey, not Turkish nationals entering Iraq. The Istanbul consulate page (mofa.gov.iq/istanbul) was not fully reviewed. Not recorded due to absence of mofa.gov.iq confirmation page.

GCC nationals (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, Oman): No official mofa.gov.iq announcement or embassy page confirms any visa-free or preferential entry arrangement for ordinary GCC passport holders entering Iraq. Wikipedia and aggregators do not list them as visa-free either.
Conditional entry rule ('15-day visa-free for US/Schengen visa holders'): No mofa.gov.iq or evisa.iq source confirms this rule. Present in travel aggregators but not verifiable from official sources.

Russia, Kuwait, Lebanon, South Korea (diplomatic/service): Wikipedia lists these as having diplomatic/service passport visa exemptions with Iraq. No mofa.gov.iq bilateral agreement announcement URLs were found for these during research. They are therefore excluded from conditional_access pending official source confirmation.
Indonesia visa exemption (September 2024): Agreement signed but no mofa.gov.iq entry-into-force date. Wikipedia notes may not yet be in force.
Malaysia visa on arrival stay duration: The mofa.gov.iq Kuala Lumpur announcement does not state maximum stay days for Malaysian visa-on-arrival holders.
China diplomatic/service/special stay duration: Not stated in the mofa.gov.iq 2022 announcement.

No official Iraqi federal government RBI or golden visa program exists. Only Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG) investor residence programs found. The gov.krd/boi-en page returned HTTP 403, so KRG details confirmed via invest.gov.krd and secondary sources.

Turkey bilateral 'joint entry visa' discussions (mofa.gov.iq August 2024 press conference) did not result in a confirmed visa-free or visa-on-arrival agreement for Turkish ordinary passport holders. The 2025 announcement (mofa.gov.iq/2025/57778) is about Iraqi nationals entering Turkey, not the reverse.
